description:
Model a hexagonal pavilion with a pyramidal roof in SketchUp Make 2017, then install solar panels on it using Skelion. 

1. Set the model's geographic location to Denver, Colorado (approx. 39.74 N, -104.99 W).
2. Use the Polygon tool to draw a 6-sided hexagon on the ground plane (circumradius 10 feet). Orient the hexagon so that one flat edge faces due south (toward the bottom/front of the default view).
3. Push/Pull the walls up to a height of 10 feet.
4. Construct a pyramidal roof that rises from the hexagonal walls to a single center peak 5 feet above the walls (total structure height 15 feet). This should create 6 triangular roof facets.
5. Open the Skelion plugin (Extensions > Skelion). Set panel dimensions to 1650mm x 992mm (standard size) in landscape orientation.
6. Insert panels ONLY on the 3 triangular roof facets that face generally southward (South, South-East, South-West).
7. Save the completed model to 'C:\Users\Docker\Documents\hexagonal_pavilion_solar.skp'.
